§6Helium-4§f

§aA fusion byproduct gas essential for quantum processing and advanced batteries!§f

§bHelium-4§f (He-4) is a gas produced in fusion reactors. Used in the production of He-4-Tritium plasma, quantum processing units, and schrabidium batteries.

§6Production§f (§bFusion Reactor Vessel§f):
§b• 20 mB§f Helium-3 → §e1,000 mB§f Helium-4
§b• 20 mB§f Deuterium → §e1,000 mB§f Helium-4
§b• 10 mB§f Deuterium + 10 mB§f Tritium → §e1,000 mB§f Helium-4

§6Key Uses§f:
§b• Quantum Processing Unit§f — Soldering Station (20s, 2 MHE/s): Atomic Clock + Versatile IC×4 + Solid State QPU×4 + PCB×16 + PVC×4 + Lead Wire×16 + §e1,000 mB He-4§f
§b• Schrabidium Battery§f — Chemical Plant (5s, 500 kHE/s): Schrabidium Powder×24 + Cast Arsenic Bronze Plate×8 + §e8,000 mB He-4§f
§b• ICF Pellet Ingredient§f — used in inertial confinement fusion fuel

§eTip§f: Helium-3 plasma is the most efficient He-4 source since it's aneutronic (0 flux). Each operation yields 1,000 mB — automate extraction to bulk storage for continuous QPU and battery production!
